(April 21, 1940 – August 9, 2010) was an American actor and one-time associate producer of *[Dark Shadows](/wiki/Dark_Shadows "Dark Shadows")*. He was in show business for over 30 years, in film, TV, stage, and commercials.[[1]](#cite_note-1) DiCenzo played Marty's grandfather Sam Baines in the film *[Back to the Future](/wiki/Back_to_the_Future "Back to the Future")*. He also had a minor role in [William Peter Blatty](/wiki/William_Peter_Blatty "William Peter Blatty")'s *[The Exorcist III](/wiki/The_Exorcist_III "The Exorcist III")*.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American actor (1940–2010)

| George DiCenzo | |
| --- | --- |
| DiCenzo in 2007 | |
| Born | George Ralph DiCenzo  (1940-04-21)April 21, 1940  [New Haven, Connecticut](/wiki/New_Haven,_Connecticut "New Haven, Connecticut"), U.S. |
| Died | August 9, 2010 (aged 70)  [Washington Crossing, Pennsylvania](/wiki/Washington_Crossing,_Pennsylvania "Washington Crossing, Pennsylvania"), U.S. |

| Spouse | Donna Artz-DiCenzo  ​ (m. 1999)​ |

**George Ralph DiCenzo** (April 21, 1940 – August 9, 2010) was an American actor and one-time associate producer of *[Dark Shadows](/wiki/Dark_Shadows "Dark Shadows")*. He was in show business for over 30 years, in film, TV, stage, and commercials.[[1]](#cite_note-1) DiCenzo played Marty's grandfather Sam Baines in the film *[Back to the Future](/wiki/Back_to_the_Future "Back to the Future")*. He also had a minor role in [William Peter Blatty](/wiki/William_Peter_Blatty "William Peter Blatty")'s *[The Exorcist III](/wiki/The_Exorcist_III "The Exorcist III")*.

## Life and career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=George_DiCenzo&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Life and career")]

DiCenzo was born in [New Haven, Connecticut](/wiki/New_Haven,_Connecticut "New Haven, Connecticut"). He appeared in more than 30 feature films, including *[Close Encounters of the Third Kind](/wiki/Close_Encounters_of_the_Third_Kind "Close Encounters of the Third Kind")* (1977), *[The Choirboys](/wiki/The_Choirboys_(film) "The Choirboys (film)")* (1977), *[The Frisco Kid](/wiki/The_Frisco_Kid "The Frisco Kid")* (1979), *[The Ninth Configuration](/wiki/The_Ninth_Configuration "The Ninth Configuration")* (1980), *[Back to the Future](/wiki/Back_to_the_Future "Back to the Future")* (1985), *[About Last Night](/wiki/About_Last_Night_(1986_film) "About Last Night (1986 film)")* (1986), *[Walk Like a Man](/wiki/Walk_Like_a_Man_(1987_film) "Walk Like a Man (1987 film)")* (1987), *[The New Adventures of Pippi Longstocking](/wiki/The_New_Adventures_of_Pippi_Longstocking "The New Adventures of Pippi Longstocking")* (1988), *[18 Again!](/wiki/18_Again! "18 Again!")* (1988), *[Sing](/wiki/Sing_(1989_film) "Sing (1989 film)")* (1989) and *[The Exorcist III](/wiki/The_Exorcist_III "The Exorcist III")* (1990).

He appeared in *[Hotel](/wiki/Hotel_(2001_film) "Hotel (2001 film)")*, directed by [Mike Figgis](/wiki/Mike_Figgis "Mike Figgis"), and *[Tempted](/wiki/Tempted_(film) "Tempted (film)")*, directed by [Bill Bennett](/wiki/Bill_Bennett_(director) "Bill Bennett (director)"). He also played the late baseball commissioner [A. Bartlett Giamatti](/wiki/A._Bartlett_Giamatti "A. Bartlett Giamatti") in the television film *[Hustle](/wiki/Hustle_(2004_film) "Hustle (2004 film)")* about disgraced baseball great [Pete Rose](/wiki/Pete_Rose "Pete Rose"). In 2002 George played [Ennio Salieri](/wiki/List_of_Mafia_characters#Ennio_Salieri "List of Mafia characters") in the 2002 video game *[Mafia](/wiki/Mafia_(video_game) "Mafia (video game)")*. George also provided the voice for Earnest Kelly in *[Grand Theft Auto: Vice City](/wiki/Grand_Theft_Auto:_Vice_City "Grand Theft Auto: Vice City")*.

In 1976, he appeared in the [CBS](/wiki/CBS "CBS") television film *[Helter Skelter](/wiki/Helter_Skelter_(1976_film) "Helter Skelter (1976 film)")* as prosecuting attorney [Vincent Bugliosi](/wiki/Vincent_Bugliosi "Vincent Bugliosi"). In the 1981–82 television season, DiCenzo was a regular on *[McClain's Law](/wiki/McClain%27s_Law "McClain's Law")*, with [James Arness](/wiki/James_Arness "James Arness") and [Marshall Colt](/wiki/Marshall_Colt "Marshall Colt"), starting with the [television film](/wiki/Television_film "Television film") *[McClain's Law](/wiki/McClain%27s_Law_(film) "McClain's Law (film)")*, structured as the series' [pilot](/wiki/Pilot_(television) "Pilot (television)"). He appeared in the 1977 miniseries *Aspen*, the 1980 television film *The Night the City Screamed*, and made regular appearances on *[Murder, She Wrote](/wiki/Murder,_She_Wrote "Murder, She Wrote")*, *[Law & Order](/wiki/Law_%26_Order "Law & Order")* and *[NYPD Blue](/wiki/NYPD_Blue "NYPD Blue")*.

His own series included *[Equal Justice](/wiki/Equal_Justice_(TV_series) "Equal Justice (TV series)")* and *[Joe's Life](/wiki/Joe%27s_Life "Joe's Life")* in the early and mid-1990s. He appeared as a guest star in the *[Law & Order: Criminal Intent](/wiki/Law_%26_Order:_Criminal_Intent "Law & Order: Criminal Intent")* episode "[Semi-Professional](/wiki/Semi-Professional_(Law_%26_Order:_Criminal_Intent_episode) "Semi-Professional (Law & Order: Criminal Intent episode)")". In the early 1990s, DiCenzo appeared on Broadway with [Nathan Lane](/wiki/Nathan_Lane "Nathan Lane") in *[On Borrowed Time](/wiki/On_Borrowed_Time "On Borrowed Time")*.

DiCenzo's voice can be heard in commercials, audiobooks, and cartoon series. His roles in the latter include the title character in *[BlackStar](/wiki/Blackstar_(TV_series) "Blackstar (TV series)")* (1981), [Hordak](/wiki/Hordak "Hordak") in *[She-Ra: Princess of Power](/wiki/She-Ra:_Princess_of_Power "She-Ra: Princess of Power")* (1985–1986), and [Captain America](/wiki/Captain_America "Captain America") in [*Spider-Man*](/wiki/Spider-Man_(1981_TV_series) "Spider-Man (1981 TV series)") (1981) and *[Spider-Man and His Amazing Friends](/wiki/Spider-Man_and_His_Amazing_Friends "Spider-Man and His Amazing Friends")* (1981–1983). He was an acting teacher in New York City and Philadelphia for several years. He apprenticed under his mentor [Milton Katselas](/wiki/Milton_Katselas "Milton Katselas") at the [Beverly Hills Playhouse](/wiki/Beverly_Hills_Playhouse "Beverly Hills Playhouse").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Death

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=George_DiCenzo&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Death")]

DiCenzo died of [sepsis](/wiki/Sepsis "Sepsis") on August 9, 2010, at the age of 70.[[3]](#cite_note-3) He was buried in the North and Southampton Churchyard at Churchville, Bucks County, Pennsylvania.

| 1993–1994 | *[Batman: The Animated Series](/wiki/Batman:_The_Animated_Series "Batman: The Animated Series")* | [Ubu](/wiki/Ubu_(comics) "Ubu (comics)") (voice) | 2 episodes[[4]](#cite_note-btva2-4) |
| 1993 | *[Joe's Life](/wiki/Joe%27s_Life "Joe's Life")* | Stan Gennaro | 11 episodes |
| 1993 | *[Animaniacs](/wiki/Animaniacs "Animaniacs")* | Commandant (voice) | Episode: "Puttin' on the Blitz"[[4]](#cite_note-btva2-4) |
| 1995–1996 | *[NYPD Blue](/wiki/NYPD_Blue "NYPD Blue")* | Pete Russell | 2 episodes |
| 1997 | *Law & Order* | Sam "Bunny" Russo | Episode: "[Barter](/wiki/Law_%26_Order_season_7#ep146 "Law & Order season 7")" |
